pages.raw_text:
Victory has been declared for the
eight hour law in Spokane. The su-
preme court at Olympia has decided
the city ordinance constitutional and
the result will be that all men en-
ployed on public contracts in this
city will work but eight hours here
after.


The decision practically ends the
proceedings instituted by Peter Co.'S
tello In the superior court recently
when he asked for an injunction to
restrain the board of public works
from cancelling the contracts on
work where he compels his men to
work nine hours. 1t is a great VIC
tory for the advocates of the eight
hour law.


The right of the state or of a
municipality to fix the number Of
hours in a days labor on public
works is clearly and effectively es-
tablished by the decision.


The case went up from Spokane.
James c. Broad was charged with
violating the eight hour ordinance,
and having been denied a writ Of
habeas corpus, he appealed to the
supreme court, claiming that the
eight hour law and all ordinances
